description: Eight new user-facing features for Omelette (Scientific Literature Lifecycle Management System) — identified through competitive analysis of open-source research tools including Paperlib (2.1k stars), Amadeus, Arxie, ScholarAIO, and ScholiumAI. Each feature addresses a real gap in omelette's current capabilities and fills needs that competing tools already serve.

userStories.title: Add bibliography export (BibTeX, RIS, EndNote) for paper collections
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to export my paper library in standard citation formats (BibTeX, RIS, EndNote XML) so I can import them into Zotero, Mendeley, LaTeX, or Word. Competitors like Paperlib and LitOrganizer support this; omelette currently has no export capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Project papers page has an 'Export' dropdown button with BibTeX, RIS, and EndNote XML options
Export respects current page filters (search, tags, status) — only filtered papers are exported
BibTeX export produces valid .bib entries with DOI, title, authors, year, journal, abstract fields
RIS export produces valid .ris format (TY, TI, AU, PY, AB, DO, ER tags)
EndNote export produces valid .xml with proper schema
Export downloads as a file automatically (not displayed on screen)
Test: export a project with 3 papers and verify BibTeX output is parseable by a BibTeX parser
Test: export respects filter — apply a tag filter and verify only matching papers appear in export
userStories.notes: Backend: add new endpoint GET /projects/{id}/export?format=bibtex|ris|endnote. Create a new export_service.py with format generators. Frontend: add Export dropdown to PapersPage toolbar. Use blob download for file delivery. No new database models needed — all data exists in Paper model.

userStories.title: Add paper notes and annotations system
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to write and organize personal notes per paper so I can capture insights, questions, and connections while reading. Competitors like Paperlib, Amadeus, and PaperArxiv all support per-paper markdown notes with LaTeX and diagram rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Each paper has a Notes tab in the paper detail/PDF reader view
Notes editor supports markdown with LaTeX math rendering (KaTeX or MathJax)
Notes are auto-saved with debounce (save after 2 seconds of inactivity)
Notes support section headers for organization (e.g., '## Summary', '## Questions', '## Key Findings')
Notes page in sidebar lists all papers that have notes for quick navigation
Test: write a note with markdown formatting and LaTeX, refresh the page, verify it persists
Test: verify auto-save indicator shows 'Saving...' then 'Saved' state
Test: verify notes are scoped to the correct paper (notes from paper A don't appear on paper B)
userStories.notes: Backend: add 'notes' field already exists on Paper model (Text column). Consider adding a separate PaperNote model with id, paper_id, content, section, order, created_at, updated_at for richer structure. Frontend: add markdown editor component (use react-markdown + KaTeX for rendering, react-simplemde-editor or similar for editing). Add Notes tab to PDFReaderPage or Paper detail panel. Auto-save via debounced API call.

userStories.title: Add reading progress tracker with reading analytics dashboard
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to track my reading progress across papers and see analytics on my reading habits so I can prioritize and measure my literature review progress. Competitors like Amadeus offer read/unread tracking and reading history with timestamps.
acceptanceCriteria: Each paper has a reading status: Unread, Reading, Read, Archived
Status can be changed via a dropdown or keyboard shortcut in the papers list
Papers list can be filtered by reading status
Dashboard view shows: total papers, papers by status (pie chart), papers read per week (bar chart), top journals read
Reading status persists across sessions in the database
Test: mark 5 papers as 'Read', verify dashboard shows 5 read papers
Test: filter papers list by 'Reading' status, verify only matching papers appear
Test: change a paper from 'Unread' to 'Read' and refresh the page, verify status persists
userStories.notes: Backend: add 'reading_status' (String) and 'read_at' (DateTime) fields to Paper model. Add GET /projects/{id}/analytics endpoint returning aggregated stats. Frontend: add reading status badge to paper rows, status filter dropdown, new Analytics sub-route under project with charts (use recharts). Keyboard shortcut: 'r' to cycle status on selected paper.

userStories.title: Add AI paper comparison view for selected papers
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to compare multiple papers side-by-side across dimensions (method, dataset, results, limitations) so I can quickly identify differences, contradictions, and complementarity. Competitors like Arxie support contradiction detection and evidence comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can select 2-5 papers from the papers list via checkboxes and click 'Compare'
Comparison view shows a table with papers as columns and comparison dimensions as rows
Dimensions include: Research Question, Method, Dataset, Key Results, Limitations, Year, Citation Count
An AI-generated summary section highlights key differences, contradictions, and complementary findings
Comparison can be regenerated with different prompts (e.g., 'Compare methods only')
Test: select 3 papers, verify comparison table renders all 5 dimensions for each paper
Test: verify AI summary contains citations back to the original papers
Test: verify selecting more than 5 papers disables the Compare button with a tooltip
userStories.notes: Backend: add POST /projects/{id}/papers/compare endpoint accepting paper_ids and optional focus dimension. Uses existing LLM provider to generate comparison from paper abstracts + OCR content. Frontend: add checkbox selection to PapersPage table, Compare button appears when 2-5 selected, new comparison dialog or full-page view with table layout. Reuse existing LLM infrastructure from writing_service.py.

userStories.title: Add research topic timeline view showing paper chronology
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to visualize papers on a chronological timeline so I can see how a research topic evolved, identify foundational papers, and track the progression of ideas over time. Competitors like Arxie support citation timeline tracing.
acceptanceCriteria: Project has a 'Timeline' tab showing all papers sorted chronologically by year/publication date
Each paper appears as a node on a horizontal timeline, sized by citation count
Papers from the same year are grouped and can be expanded to show details
Hovering a paper shows a tooltip with title, authors, journal, citation count, and a one-line AI summary
Clicking a paper navigates to its detail/PDF reader view
Timeline can be filtered by tags or search query
Test: create a project with papers from 2019-2024, verify timeline renders all years correctly
Test: filter by a tag, verify only matching papers appear on timeline
Test: verify timeline is scrollable and handles 50+ papers without performance issues
userStories.notes: Frontend-only feature using existing paper data (year, citation_count fields). Create new TimelineView component with a custom horizontal timeline. Use d3 or a lightweight timeline library. AI summary on hover can be generated on-demand or pre-cached. Group papers by year, sort by citation count within each year.

userStories.title: Add paper rating and quality scoring system
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to rate papers (1-5 stars) and assign custom quality tags (e.g., 'seminal', 'controversial', 'survey') so I can prioritize high-quality papers and filter my library by perceived importance. Competitors like Paperlib support rating, flagging, and tagging.
acceptanceCriteria: Each paper in the list view has a 5-star rating component (click to rate, hover to preview)
Rating is saved immediately to the database
Papers list can be sorted by rating (highest first)
Users can add custom quality tags from a predefined set (Seminal, Survey, Controversial, Replication, Methodology) plus custom tags
Papers can be filtered by quality tags
Rating and tags are visible as columns in the papers table
Test: rate a paper 4 stars, refresh page, verify rating persists
Test: add a quality tag 'Seminal' to a paper, filter by that tag, verify only matching papers appear
Test: sort papers by rating descending, verify highest-rated papers appear first
userStories.notes: Backend: add 'rating' (Integer, 0-5) and 'quality_tags' (JSON array) fields to Paper model. Add query params for sort=rating and filter by quality_tags. Frontend: add star rating component (can use shadcn/ui or build custom), quality tag dropdown/badges. Update PapersPage table to include rating column and tag filters. Sort and filter integration with existing paperApi.list params.

userStories.title: Add related paper recommendations based on semantic similarity
userStories.description: As a researcher reading a paper, I need to discover semantically related papers in my library so I don't miss relevant work I've already collected. This leverages the existing ChromaDB vector store that already holds paper embeddings for RAG queries.
acceptanceCriteria: Paper detail/PDF reader view shows a 'Related Papers' section listing 5-10 most similar papers from the same project
Each related paper shows title, authors, year, and a similarity score (0-100%)
Similarity is computed using existing ChromaDB embeddings (cosine similarity)
Clicking a related paper navigates to its detail view
Related papers section has a 'Find more like this' action that triggers a broader semantic search across all projects
Test: verify related papers for a given paper are semantically similar (not random)
Test: verify clicking a related paper navigates correctly
Test: verify 'Find more like this' returns papers from outside the current project
userStories.notes: Backend: add GET /projects/{id}/papers/{paper_id}/similar?limit=10 endpoint. Use existing embedding_service.py and ChromaDB to query similar vectors. Frontend: add RelatedPapers card component to PDFReaderPage or paper detail panel. Display similarity score as a percentage badge. 'Find more like this' reuses the semantic search from knowledge bases.

userStories.title: Add project activity feed and change log
userStories.description: As a researcher working across multiple sessions, I need an activity feed showing recent actions (papers added, notes written, status changes, RAG indexing) so I can quickly pick up where I left off. This provides a 'what happened since I was last here' overview that no competitor currently offers.
acceptanceCriteria: Project detail page has an 'Activity' tab showing a chronological feed of recent actions
Activity types include: paper_added, paper_deleted, note_updated, status_changed, rag_indexed, writing_generated, search_executed, keyword_added
Each activity entry shows: action type icon, description, timestamp, and a link to the relevant paper/page
Feed is paginated (show 20 entries, load more on scroll)
Activity feed highlights entries since the user's last visit (e.g., '5 new activities since yesterday')
Activity data is stored in the database with a new activity_log table
Test: add a paper, update a note, change a status — verify all three appear in the activity feed
Test: verify pagination loads older entries correctly
Test: verify 'since last visit' highlighting works (clear last_visited, add activities, verify they're highlighted)
userStories.notes: Backend: create new ActivityLog model (id, project_id, action_type, entity_type, entity_id, description, created_at). Add event hooks in existing services to write activity entries (paper_service, rag_service, writing_service, etc.). Add GET /projects/{id}/activity?limit=20&cursor= endpoint. Frontend: add ActivityFeed component with grouped timeline entries (group by date). Use intersection observer for infinite scroll. Add 'last_visited' tracking to user_settings model.
